title: County Of San Diego Instructional Service Guide Ssd Commercial
text:
All food and 
beverage containers 
should be EMPTY: 
no/minimal food 
residue and no 
liquids remaining, 
and boxes should 
be broken down 
and flattened to fit 
inside the recycling 
container.
RECYCLING
Use your blue recycling container to recycle household 
containers, mixed paper, newspaper, cardboard and 
beverage containers marked 
 - 
, labeled “CA 
Redemption Value” or “CA Cash Refund,” all mixed 
together in your recycling cart or bin. Please help prevent 
contamination by not placing any trash or non-recyclable 
items in your blue recycling container. DO NOT use 
plastic bags! Recyclable material must be placed loosely 
inside recycling containers only. 
edcodisposal.com
edcodisposal.com
•	Soiled Disposable Plates or Cups
•	Soiled Paper Towels
•	Soiled Tissue
•	Plastic Bags (NO plastic grocery bags, 
newspaper delivery bags or chip/snack bags)
•	Ceramics, Drinking Glasses, or Mirrors
•	Fluorescent Light Bulbs
•	Electronics or Batteries
•	Household Hazardous Waste (HHW)
•	Clothing or Shoes
KEEP OUT OF THE RECYCLING CONTAINER: 
RECYCLABLES INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:
CARTON CONTAINERS: 
Aseptic cartons, including milk cartons 
and juice boxes, half-gallon juice cartons, 
soup/broth cartons, and wine cartons.
CARDBOARD:
Cardboard, including boxes used for moving and 
shipping or any other box or packaging made of 
corrugated cardboard.
GLASS CONTAINERS: 
Glass containers including food jars 
and beverage bottles.
MIXED PAPER:
Mixed paper, including junk 
mail, catalogs, magazines, 
newspapers, and paper used 
for computer printing.
PAPERBOARD:
Paperboard boxes such 
as cereal boxes, tissue 
boxes, refrigerated 
food boxes, boxes from 
toiletries, and soda/beer 
cases.
SHREDDED 
PAPER: 
Shredded paper 
(placed in a sealed 
clear plastic bag  for 
easier handling).
STYROFOAM:
Clean foam cups, meat trays, 
plates, egg cartons, block 
packaging (no peanuts), rigid 
polystyrene, and carry-out 
containers.
